resources.string: No se logro eliminar el registro
No se logro actualizar la proyección

Act as a Java Regex compiler.
Analyze the SMS message and generate a single, linear regular expression that captures: merchant/product name, value, and date.
Technical Escaping Instructions (CRITICAL):
Every dollar sign "$" must be written as "\$". Every forward slash "/" must be written as "\/". Every dot "." that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\.". Every comma "," that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\,". Every asteric "*" that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\*".
Use capture groups () with specific character classes: ([\w ]+) for the merchant/product name. ([\d\.\,]+) for the value. (\d{2}\/\d{2}\/\d{4}) for the date but it depend of date format ex. dd/mm/yyyy.
Do not use generic wildcards like ".+?"; use text anchors from the message and [\w ]+ connectors.
The structure must be compatible with java.util.regex.Pattern.
Response Rules:
Provide ONLY the raw text string. Do not use code blocks (```). Do not use quotation marks. Do not provide explanations or introductions. If the result does not include the escape characters "" before "$" and "/", the response will be considered incorrect.
Message: %s

Act as a Java Regex compiler.
Analyze the Email body and generate a single, linear regular expression that captures: merchant/product name, value, and date.
Technical Escaping Instructions (CRITICAL):
Every dollar sign "$" must be written as "\$". Every forward slash "/" must be written as "\/". Every dot "." that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\.". Every comma "," that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\,". Every asteric "*" that must be treated as a literal must be written as "\*".
Use capture groups () with specific names: (?<name>[\w ]+) for the merchant/product name. (?<value>[\d\.\,]+) for the value. (?<date>\d{2}\/\d{2}\/\d{4}) for the date but it depend of date format ex. dd/mm/yyyy.
Do not use generic wildcards like ".+?"; use text anchors from the message and [\w ]+ connectors.
The structure must be compatible with java.util.regex.Pattern.
Response Rules:
Provide ONLY the raw text string for SUBJECT and BODY. SUBJECT: (regex for email subject) BODY: (regex for email body) Do not use code blocks (```). Do not use quotation marks. Do not provide explanations or introductions. If the result does not include the escape characters "" before "$" and "/", the response will be considered incorrect.
Email Samples: %s
Proceso de lectura de email completado
